This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Takakurayama Kofun | image = [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 03.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber (1978) | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Toyokawa-cho
(within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}) | coordinates = 34|28|55.70 | coordinates = 136|41|56.83 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter over 35m
Height 7.5m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama-style chamber) | burial goods = Gold rings, beads, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Late 6th century | Occupant = (Possibly) a chief of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Ise City Designated Historic Site "Takakurayama Kofun" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Takakurayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Takakurayama Kofun''' (高倉山古墳) is a Kofun located in Toyokawa-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). The mound is Historic Site1 as an Ise City Historic Site. The kofun is renowned for housing one of Japan's largest {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, but entry is restricted as it lies within the precincts of Toyouke Daijingu. == Overview == [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 02.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Takakurayama, where the kofun is located}}]] The kofun is situated atop {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}} (elevation 116 meters), within the sacred precincts of the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. The mound has been mentioned in historical documents dating back to the Kamakura period and was widely venerated during the Edo period as a {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). This veneration led to significant alterations to the mound due to erosion and flattening. Surveys and excavations were conducted in 1975. The tumulus is believed to have been circular, with an estimated diameter of over 35 meters (possibly over 40 meters) and a height of approximately 7.5 meters (or 8.5 meters). No evidence of stone coverings or haniwa has been found on the surface. The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the southwest. With a total length of 18.5 meters, it is the largest stone chamber in the Ise region and ranks sixth nationwide in length and second in floor area of the main chamber. burial goods include gold rings, beads, horse fittings, swords, and pottery. The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} * {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Mie Prefecture]] revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Takakurayama Kofun | image = [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 03.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber (1978) | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Toyokawa-cho
(within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}) | coordinates = 34|28|55.70 | coordinates = 136|41|56.83 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter over 35m
Height 7.5m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama-style chamber) | burial goods = Gold rings, beads, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Late 6th century | Occupant = (Possibly) a chief of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Ise City Designated Historic Site "Takakurayama Kofun" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Takakurayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Takakurayama Kofun''' (高倉山古墳) is a Kofun located in Toyokawa-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). The mound is Historic Site1 as an Ise City Historic Site. The kofun is renowned for housing one of Japan's largest {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, but entry is restricted as it lies within the precincts of Toyouke Daijingu. == Overview == [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 02.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Takakurayama, where the kofun is located}}]] The kofun is situated atop {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}} (elevation 116 meters), within the sacred precincts of the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. The mound has been mentioned in historical documents dating back to the Kamakura period and was widely venerated during the Edo period as a {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). This veneration led to significant alterations to the mound due to erosion and flattening. Surveys and excavations were conducted in 1975. The tumulus is believed to have been circular, with an estimated diameter of over 35 meters (possibly over 40 meters) and a height of approximately 7.5 meters (or 8.5 meters). No evidence of stone coverings or haniwa has been found on the surface. The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the southwest. With a total length of 18.5 meters, it is the largest stone chamber in the Ise region and ranks sixth nationwide in length and second in floor area of the main chamber. burial goods include gold rings, beads, horse fittings, swords, and pottery. The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} * {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai clan|Kofun]] [[Category:Takakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[ja:高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:akakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[en:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]]

The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai clan|Kofun]] [[Category:Takakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[ja:高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:akakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[en:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Takakurayama Kofun | image = [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 03.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber (1978) | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, Toyokawa-cho
(within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}) | coordinates = 34|28|55.70 | coordinates = 136|41|56.83 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter over 35m
Height 7.5m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama-style chamber) | burial goods = Gold rings, beads, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Late 6th century | Occupant = (Possibly) a chief of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Ise City Designated Historic Site "Takakurayama Kofun" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Takakurayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Takakurayama Kofun''' (高倉山古墳) is a Kofun located in Toyokawa-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}). The mound is Historic Site1 as an Ise City Historic Site. The kofun is renowned for housing one of Japan's largest {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, but entry is restricted as it lies within the precincts of Toyouke Daijingu. == Overview == [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 02.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Takakurayama, where the kofun is located}}]] The kofun is situated atop {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} (elevation 116 meters), within the sacred precincts of the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. The mound has been mentioned in historical documents dating back to the Kamakura period and was widely venerated during the Edo period as a {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). This veneration led to significant alterations to the mound due to erosion and flattening. Surveys and excavations were conducted in 1975. The tumulus is believed to have been circular, with an estimated diameter of over 35 meters (possibly over 40 meters) and a height of approximately 7.5 meters (or 8.5 meters). No evidence of stone coverings or haniwa has been found on the surface. The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the southwest. With a total length of 18.5 meters, it is the largest stone chamber in the Ise region and ranks sixth nationwide in length and second in floor area of the main chamber. burial goods include gold rings, beads, horse fittings, swords, and pottery. The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai clan|Kofun]] [[Category:Takakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[ja:高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:akakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[en:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]]

Height 7.5m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama-style chamber) | burial goods = Gold rings, beads, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Late 6th century | Occupant = (Possibly) a chief of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Ise City Designated Historic Site "Takakurayama Kofun" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Takakurayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Takakurayama Kofun''' (高倉山古墳) is a Kofun located in Toyokawa-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}). The mound is Historic Site1 as an Ise City Historic Site. The kofun is renowned for housing one of Japan's largest {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, but entry is restricted as it lies within the precincts of Toyouke Daijingu. == Overview == [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 02.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Takakurayama, where the kofun is located}}]] The kofun is situated atop {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} (elevation 116 meters), within the sacred precincts of the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. The mound has been mentioned in historical documents dating back to the Kamakura period and was widely venerated during the Edo period as a {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). This veneration led to significant alterations to the mound due to erosion and flattening. Surveys and excavations were conducted in 1975. The tumulus is believed to have been circular, with an estimated diameter of over 35 meters (possibly over 40 meters) and a height of approximately 7.5 meters (or 8.5 meters). No evidence of stone coverings or haniwa has been found on the surface. The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the southwest. With a total length of 18.5 meters, it is the largest stone chamber in the Ise region and ranks sixth nationwide in length and second in floor area of the main chamber. burial goods include gold rings, beads, horse fittings, swords, and pottery. The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai clan|Kofun]] [[Category:Takakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[ja:高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:akakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[en:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] {{Kofun navbar}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Takakurayama Kofun | image = [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 03.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber (1978) | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, Toyokawa-cho
(within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}) | coordinates = 34|28|55.70 | coordinates = 136|41|56.83 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter over 35m
Height 7.5m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama-style chamber) | burial goods = Gold rings, beads, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Late 6th century | Occupant = (Possibly) a chief of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Ise City Designated Historic Site "Takakurayama Kofun" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Takakurayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Takakurayama Kofun''' (高倉山古墳) is a Kofun located in Toyokawa-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}). The mound is Historic Site1 as an Ise City Historic Site. The kofun is renowned for housing one of Japan's largest {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, but entry is restricted as it lies within the precincts of Toyouke Daijingu. == Overview == [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 02.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Takakurayama, where the kofun is located}}]] The kofun is situated atop {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} (elevation 116 meters), within the sacred precincts of the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. The mound has been mentioned in historical documents dating back to the Kamakura period and was widely venerated during the Edo period as a {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). This veneration led to significant alterations to the mound due to erosion and flattening. Surveys and excavations were conducted in 1975. The tumulus is believed to have been circular, with an estimated diameter of over 35 meters (possibly over 40 meters) and a height of approximately 7.5 meters (or 8.5 meters). No evidence of stone coverings or haniwa has been found on the surface. The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the southwest. With a total length of 18.5 meters, it is the largest stone chamber in the Ise region and ranks sixth nationwide in length and second in floor area of the main chamber. burial goods include gold rings, beads, horse fittings, swords, and pottery. The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai clan|Kofun]] [[Category:Takakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[ja:高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:akakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[en:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai clan|Kofun]] [[Category:Takakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[ja:高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:akakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[en:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Takakurayama Kofun | image = [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 03.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber (1978) | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, Toyokawa-cho
(within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}) | coordinates = 34|28|55.70 | coordinates = 136|41|56.83 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter over 35m
Height 7.5m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama-style chamber) | burial goods = Gold rings, beads, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Late 6th century | Occupant = (Possibly) a chief of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Ise City Designated Historic Site "Takakurayama Kofun" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Takakurayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Takakurayama Kofun''' (高倉山古墳) is a Kofun located in Toyokawa-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}). The mound is Historic Site1 as an Ise City Historic Site. The kofun is renowned for housing one of Japan's largest {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, but entry is restricted as it lies within the precincts of Toyouke Daijingu. == Overview == [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 02.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Takakurayama, where the kofun is located}}]] The kofun is situated atop {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} (elevation 116 meters), within the sacred precincts of the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. The mound has been mentioned in historical documents dating back to the Kamakura period and was widely venerated during the Edo period as a {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). This veneration led to significant alterations to the mound due to erosion and flattening. Surveys and excavations were conducted in 1975. The tumulus is believed to have been circular, with an estimated diameter of over 35 meters (possibly over 40 meters) and a height of approximately 7.5 meters (or 8.5 meters). No evidence of stone coverings or haniwa has been found on the surface. The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the southwest. With a total length of 18.5 meters, it is the largest stone chamber in the Ise region and ranks sixth nationwide in length and second in floor area of the main chamber. burial goods include gold rings, beads, horse fittings, swords, and pottery. The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai clan|Kofun]] [[Category:Takakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[ja:高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:akakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] [[en:Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

The kofun is renowned for housing one of Japan's largest {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, but entry is restricted as it lies within the precincts of Toyouke Daijingu. == Overview == [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 02.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Takakurayama, where the kofun is located}}]] The kofun is situated atop {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} (elevation 116 meters), within the sacred precincts of the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. The mound has been mentioned in historical documents dating back to the Kamakura period and was widely venerated during the Edo period as a {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). This veneration led to significant alterations to the mound due to erosion and flattening. Surveys and excavations were conducted in 1975. The tumulus is believed to have been circular, with an estimated diameter of over 35 meters (possibly over 40 meters) and a height of approximately 7.5 meters (or 8.5 meters). No evidence of stone coverings or haniwa has been found on the surface. The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the southwest. With a total length of 18.5 meters, it is the largest stone chamber in the Ise region and ranks sixth nationwide in length and second in floor area of the main chamber. burial goods include gold rings, beads, horse fittings, swords, and pottery. The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakurayama Kofun Entrance to the stone chamber (1978) | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, Toyokawa-cho
(within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}) | coordinates = 34|28|55.70 | coordinates = 136|41|56.83 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter over 35m
Height 7.5m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama-style chamber) | burial goods = Gold rings, beads, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Late 6th century | Occupant = (Possibly) a chief of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Ise City Designated Historic Site "Takakurayama Kofun" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Takakurayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Takakurayama Kofun''' (高倉山古墳) is a Kofun located in Toyokawa-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}). The mound is Historic Site1 as an Ise City Historic Site. The kofun is renowned for housing one of Japan's largest {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, but entry is restricted as it lies within the precincts of Toyouke Daijingu. == Overview == [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 02.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Takakurayama, where the kofun is located}}]] The kofun is situated atop {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} (elevation 116 meters), within the sacred precincts of the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. The mound has been mentioned in historical documents dating back to the Kamakura period and was widely venerated during the Edo period as a {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). This veneration led to significant alterations to the mound due to erosion and flattening. Surveys and excavations were conducted in 1975. The tumulus is believed to have been circular, with an estimated diameter of over 35 meters (possibly over 40 meters) and a height of approximately 7.5 meters (or 8.5 meters). No evidence of stone coverings or haniwa has been found on the surface. The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the southwest. With a total length of 18.5 meters, it is the largest stone chamber in the Ise region and ranks sixth nationwide in length and second in floor area of the main chamber. burial goods include gold rings, beads, horse fittings, swords, and pottery. The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Takakurayama Kofun | image = [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 03.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber (1978) | alternate_name = | Cluster = | location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, Toyokawa-cho
(within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}) | coordinates = 34|28|55.70 | coordinates = 136|41|56.83 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter over 35m
Height 7.5m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama-style chamber) | burial goods = Gold rings, beads, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Late 6th century | Occupant = (Possibly) a chief of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Ise City Designated Historic Site "Takakurayama Kofun" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Takakurayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Takakurayama Kofun''' (高倉山古墳) is a Kofun located in Toyokawa-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}). The mound is Historic Site1 as an Ise City Historic Site. The kofun is renowned for housing one of Japan's largest {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, but entry is restricted as it lies within the precincts of Toyouke Daijingu. == Overview == [[File:Takakura-yama(Ise) 02.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Takakurayama, where the kofun is located}}]] The kofun is situated atop {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} (elevation 116 meters), within the sacred precincts of the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. The mound has been mentioned in historical documents dating back to the Kamakura period and was widely venerated during the Edo period as a {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). This veneration led to significant alterations to the mound due to erosion and flattening. Surveys and excavations were conducted in 1975. The tumulus is believed to have been circular, with an estimated diameter of over 35 meters (possibly over 40 meters) and a height of approximately 7.5 meters (or 8.5 meters). No evidence of stone coverings or haniwa has been found on the surface. The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the southwest. With a total length of 18.5 meters, it is the largest stone chamber in the Ise region and ranks sixth nationwide in length and second in floor area of the main chamber. burial goods include gold rings, beads, horse fittings, swords, and pottery. The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}}
