The tumulus is believed to have been circular, with an estimated diameter of over 35 meters (possibly over 40 meters) and a height of approximately 7.5 meters (or 8.5 meters). No evidence of stone coverings or haniwa has been found on the surface. The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the southwest. With a total length of 18.5 meters, it is the largest stone chamber in the Ise region and Ranks sixth nationwide in length and second in floor area of the main chamber. burial goods include gold rings, beads, horse fittings, swords, and pottery. The mound is believed to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until the early 7th century. == Excavation History == * '''Kamakura period''': Mentioned in the ''Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki'', ''Tenchi Reikiki'', and ''Kokuzo-no-hisho'', referring to a stone cave on Takakurayama. * '''Muromachi period''': The ''Jingi Hisho'' records the area of the stone chamber. * '''Edo period''': Venerated as {{ill|Amano-Iwato|en|Amano-Iwato|WD=Q3231835|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|1=Amano-Iwato}}, with a kagura hall built on the northern slope of the mound and tea shops nearby. * '''1888''': Observed by archaeologist Masugoro Tsuboi, recognizing it as a kofun. * '''1914''': Detailed drawings of the chamber created by Yasunosuke Seki, Genichi Ōnishi, and others. * '''1973''': Designated as an Ise City Historic Site. * '''1975''': Excavation and cleaning of the stone chamber. == burial == The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 18.5 meters * Main chamber: Length 9.7 meters, width 3.3 meters, height 4.1 meters * Corridor: Length 8.8 meters, width 1.9–2.6 meters, height 3.3 meters The chamber walls consist of natural stones arranged in five to six courses atop base stones. The chamber is architecturally distinctive, sharing traits with both the Kinki region and the western Mikawa region across Ise Bay. == burial goods == burial goods recovered during the 1975 excavation include: * Ornaments: Gold rings, tubular beads, magatama, glass beads, and cut beads. * Weapons: Swords, daggers, and iron arrowheads. * Horse fittings: Bridle parts, decorative fittings, and saddle fittings. * Pottery: Various {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} types. burial goods suggest the mound was reused for additional burials into the early 7th century. == Cultural Significance == === Ise City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: Takakurayama Kofun. Designated December 5, 1973. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Takakurayama Kofun|title=History of Mie Prefecture: Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2011|chapter=Special Topics > Takakurayama Kofun|title=Ise City History Volume 6: Archaeology|publisher=Ise City|ref={{Harvid|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise History)|2011}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not directly cited in this article:}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=Ise City’s Takakurayama Kofun|title=Fubito Volume 24|publisher=Archaeological Society of Mie University|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takakurayama (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama (Ise)|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|version=99995230|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q85884614}} [[Category:History of Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Watarai Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
